name: Quality Tier 8 — anti-joins / EXISTS / NULL semantics
description: Absence checks: 'X with no Y' / 'X without any Y' (anti-join) and 'X with NULL field' (is_null filter). Tests the orchestrator's anti-join routing and the FK is-null vs anti-join distinction.

Scenarios.Question: service types that have not had any outage
Scenarios.ExpectedShape: SELECT ServiceTypes.NameEn ANTI JOIN Outages — 1 row (only 1 service type has 0 outages)
Scenarios.Notes: Classic anti-join. 5 service types total, 4 with outages, so result is 1 row.

Scenarios.Question: departments that have never received a ticket
Scenarios.ExpectedShape: SELECT Departments.NameEn ANTI JOIN Tickets — 24 of 60 depts have 0 tickets
Scenarios.Notes: Larger anti-join result. Tests that the anti-join correctly produces non-matching rows.

Scenarios.Question: tickets that have no assignee
Scenarios.ExpectedShape: SELECT Tickets.TicketNumber WHERE AssignedToUserId IS NULL — 83 rows (all tickets are unassigned in this DB)
Scenarios.Notes: is_null on an FK column (NOT an anti-join — the worked example explicitly disambiguates).
